title: Procurement Specialist

rationales.taskAutomation: Purchase-order creation, catalog management, and routine vendor comparison are largely automatable; strategic sourcing and contract negotiation are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI procurement platforms with spend-analysis and vendor-matching are credible and improving fast, though not yet as embedded as mainstream coding or writing copilots.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is common at large enterprises with mature procurement functions, spreading toward mid-market.
rationales.agenticExposure: Routine PO processing and invoice-matching chains are agent-ownable; complex negotiation and supplier-risk judgment remain human.

tasksAtRisk: Vendor comparison for standard, non-strategic items
tasksGrowing: Supply chain risk assessment and mitigation
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1023 (Purchasing Agents, Except Wholesale, Retail, and Farm Products), p25-p75 rounded
insight: AI now handles the transactional half of procurement — POs, catalog pricing, invoice matching — leaving negotiation and supplier risk as the durable core. Specialists who move up into strategic sourcing hold a clearly defensible High-but-not-Very-High position.
